name: troubleshooting
context: Debug missing spans, metrics, logs, propagation, OTLP endpoints, Collector pipelines, and sampling mistakes.
info: OpenTelemetry failures are usually configuration or pipeline failures: the SDK starts too late, the service name is missing, the exporter points at the wrong endpoint, HTTP paths are wrong, gRPC is blocked, context propagation is not crossing a boundary, Collector pipelines omit a signal, processors drop data, or sampling removes the trace. Agents use this node as the operational checklist before rewriting instrumentation or blaming the backend.
body:
# OpenTelemetry Troubleshooting

Use this checklist in order. Most issues are found before application code needs to change.

## One-Minute Checks

```bash
# Service identity
printf '%s\n' "$OTEL_SERVICE_NAME" "$OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES"

# Endpoint and protocol
printf '%s\n' "$OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT" "$OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL"

# Collector health and internal metrics
curl -fsS http://localhost:13133/ || true
curl -fsS http://localhost:8888/metrics | head || true
```

## Common Failure Modes

| Symptom | Likely cause | Fix |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `unknown_service` | Missing `service.name` | Set `OTEL_SERVICE_NAME` or resource attributes |
| No spans at all | SDK not initialized before app imports | Start Node with `node --import ./instrumentation.mjs app.js` |
| OTLP/HTTP 404 | Wrong endpoint path | Use base `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://host:4318` or signal URL with `/v1/traces` |
| gRPC unavailable | Proxy or network blocks HTTP/2 | Switch to `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL=http/protobuf` and port `4318` |
| Split traces | Context not propagated | Check `traceparent` headers or queue metadata propagation |
| Only root spans | Auto-instrumentation loaded too late | Register instrumentation before loading HTTP, DB, or framework modules |
| Errors missing | Span status not set or sampling drops trace | Record exceptions and review [[opentelemetry/sampling]] |
| Metrics explode | High-cardinality attributes | Remove IDs, UUIDs, emails, full URLs, raw SQL |

## Collector Debug Exporter

Add a debug exporter while verifying pipeline shape:

```yaml
exporters:
  debug:
    verbosity: detailed
service:
  pipelines:
    traces:
      receivers: [otlp]
      processors: [batch]
      exporters: [debug]
```

If the debug exporter prints data, the SDK and receiver work. If the backend still shows nothing, inspect exporter auth, endpoint, TLS, and vendor-specific limits.

sections.title: SDK startup order
sections.body: Auto-instrumentation must register before instrumented modules are imported. In Node.js, prefer `node --import ./instrumentation.mjs app.js` for ESM startup. If the app imports Express, HTTP, database clients, or queue clients before the SDK starts, spans may be missing or incomplete. See [[opentelemetry/nodejs#startup-order]].

sections.title: Endpoint and protocol
sections.body: Check port and transport first. `4317` is usually OTLP/gRPC, and `4318` is usually OTLP/HTTP. For general HTTP endpoint variables, SDKs append `/v1/traces`, `/v1/metrics`, or `/v1/logs`; signal-specific endpoint variables are used as-is. See [[opentelemetry/otlp#ports-and-paths]].

sections.title: Collector pipelines
sections.body: A Collector component is inert until referenced from `service.pipelines` or `service.extensions`. Make sure each signal has a pipeline and that the pipeline references the intended receivers, processors, and exporters. Check collector logs and `otelcol_*` internal metrics for dropped data or exporter failures.

sections.title: Propagation
sections.body: For HTTP, inspect the inbound and outbound `traceparent` header. For queues and custom protocols, make sure context is injected into metadata on publish and extracted on consume. Be careful with public or untrusted boundaries: sanitize or ignore incoming context when needed, and never propagate secrets through baggage.

links.context: Fix Node.js startup order and SDK setup
Inspect collector pipelines, logs, health, and debug exporter
Verify endpoints, ports, paths, protocol, headers, and TLS
Fix missing or unstable service and span attributes
Use curl to inspect health endpoints and HTTP collector responses
Post-process collector or backend JSON responses

related.note: Endpoint and protocol errors are among the most common OTLP failures
The collector exposes logs, health endpoints, and internal metrics for diagnosis
HTTP checks are often the fastest way to isolate endpoint issues

author: dojo-community
